IN THE NAME OF THE RUSSIAN FEDERATION
September 02, 2024 Tsentralno-City District Court of Makeevka, Donetsk People's Republic, consisting of:
presiding judge             Vorobey E.L.,
under the secretary                            Velichko N.A.,

In accordance with paragraphs. 2.9.3 clause 2.9 of the Procedure “On the identification, registration and acceptance into municipal ownership of ownerless real estate and escheat property”, approved by Decree of the Head of the Donetsk People's Republic No. 116 of April 28, 2021 (hereinafter referred to as Procedure No. 116), the City Administration carried out all the prescribed measures to identify real estate that has signs ownerless and subject to the Standard Procedure. After the expiration of a three-month period from the date of registration of the Object as ownerless, any individuals and legal entities did not declare any claims regarding the Object that is the subject of this application, that is, there is no dispute about the right.

According to paragraph 3 of Art. 218 of the Civil Code of the Russian Federation, in cases and in the manner provided for by this Code, a person may acquire ownership of property that does not have an owner, of property whose owner is unknown, or of property that the owner has abandoned or to which he has lost ownership on other grounds provided by law.
The provisions of Art. 225 of the Civil Code of the Russian Federation establishes that the ownerlessness of real estate presupposes the absence or unknownness of its owner, and also the condition for considering an application to recognize the property as ownerless is the absence of a dispute about the right.
In accordance with paragraph 1 of Art. 225 of the Civil Code of the Russian Federation, ownerless is a thing that does not have an owner or the owner of which is unknown or, unless otherwise provided by law, the right of ownership to which the owner has refused.
By virtue of clause 3 of Art. 225 of the Civil Code of the Russian Federation, ownerless real estate is accepted for registration by the body that carries out state registration of rights to real estate, upon application from the local government body in whose territory they are located. After a year has passed from the date of registration of an ownerless immovable property, and in the case of registration of a linear object, after three months from the date of registration, the body authorized to manage municipal property may apply to the court with a request to recognize the right of municipal ownership of this thing.
According to Part 7 of Art. 12 of the Federal Constitutional Law “On the admission of the Donetsk People's Republic to the Russian Federation and the formation of a new subject within the Russian Federation - the Donetsk People's Republic No. 5-FKZ dated 10/04/2022, management of property located on the territory of the Donetsk People's Republic, which has signs of ownerlessness and is not classified as federal property, the property of a subject of the Russian Federation or municipal property, is carried out by the authorities of the Donetsk People's Republic in in the manner established by the Government of the Russian Federation, unless otherwise provided by this Federal Constitutional Law. The procedure for recognizing such property as ownerless, established by the regulatory legal acts of the Donetsk People's Republic, is applied until January 1, 2028, in agreement with the federal executive body exercising the functions of managing state property.
By Decree of the Acting Head of the Donetsk People's Republic dated 02.27.2023 No. 64 “On some issues related to the management of property that has signs of ownerlessness, located on the territory of the Donetsk People’s Republic, and the recognition of such property as ownerless,” the Government of the Donetsk People’s Republic is vested with the authority to establish (adopt) a procedure for recognizing as ownerless property that has signs of ownerlessness and not classified as federal property, property of a constituent entity of the Russian Federation or municipal property located on the territory of the Donetsk People's Republic, with the exception of real estate in the form of residential premises, residential buildings, and outbuildings.
In accordance with Art. 10 of the Law of the Donetsk People's Republic "On the peculiarities of identifying, using and recognizing the right of municipal property of municipalities of the Donetsk People's Republic to residential premises with signs of ownerless property located on the territory of the Donetsk People's Republic" dated 03.21.2024 No. 66-RZ after three months from the date of designation of the residential premises as ownerless by the territorial body of the federal executive body carrying out state cadastral registration, state registration of rights, maintenance of the Unified State Register of Real Estate, the authorized body applies to the court with an application for recognition of the right of municipal property of a municipal entity to this residential premises in the manner prescribed by the legislation of the Russian Federation.
Decree of the Acting Head of the Donetsk People's Republic dated May 10, 2023 No. 140            (hereinafter referred to as Decree No. 140) established the specifics of acquiring state and municipal property rights to ownerless real estate on the territory of the Donetsk People's Republic.
According to clause 1.2 of Decree No. 140, the executive body of the Donetsk People's Republic authorized to manage state property has the right to apply to the court with a demand for recognition of the right of state ownership of the objects defined in subclause 1.1 of this clause (with the exception of residential premises, outbuildings intended to satisfy citizens' household and other needs), after three months from the date of their registration as ownerless real estate by the body, carrying out state registration of rights to real estate.
Thus, legal relations related to the recognition of real estate as ownerless are regulated by regulatory legal acts of the Donetsk People's Republic.
According to Part 2 of Art. 290 of the Code of Civil Procedure of the Russian Federation, an application for recognition of the right of municipal ownership of an ownerless immovable property is submitted to the court at its location by the body authorized to manage municipal property.
According to Part 2 of Art. 293 of the Code of Civil Procedure of the Russian Federation, the court, having recognized that the immovable thing does not have an owner or the owner of the immovable thing is unknown and it was registered in the prescribed manner, makes a decision to recognize the right of municipal property or the property of the federal city of Moscow, St. Petersburg or Sevastopol to this thing.
Having assessed the evidence examined at the court hearing, the court comes to the conclusion that there is a unified procedure for identifying, registering, storing and accepting into municipal ownership ownerless real estate and escheat property identified on the territory of the relevant administrative-territorial unit of the Donetsk People's Republic, provided for by the Procedure “On the identification, registration and acceptance into municipal ownership of ownerless real estate and escheat property”, approved by the Decree of the Head of the Donetsk People's Republic No. 116 of 04/28/2021 is observed that any individuals and legal entities, including
Full name5

,

<address>

<address>

<address>
, does not have an owner, considers it possible to satisfy the stated requirements and recognize the municipal formation of the Makeyevka Urban District of the Donetsk People's Republic as having the right of municipal ownership of the specified real estate property.
Guided by Art. Art. 195, 198, 262, part 2 art. 293 of the Civil Procedure Code of the Russian Federation, court
DECISION:
The application of the Administration of the Makeyevka urban district of the Donetsk People's Republic is satisfied.
Recognize the right of municipal ownership of residential premises - an apartment located at the address:
Full name1
,
Full name2
Republic, Makeevka urban district,
<address>
, cadastral
No.
, for the municipality - the Makeevka Urban District of the Donetsk People's Republic.
An appeal against the decision can be filed with the Supreme Court of the Donetsk People's Republic through the Tsentralno-Gorodsky District Court of Makeevka, Donetsk People's Republic, within a month from the date the decision was made in final form.
The reasoned decision was made on September 12, 2024.
Judge                                   E.L. Sparrow
